title: Skirmish on Ord Mantell (0.5 ABY)

{{Quote|Maybe it was just bad luck that he was there, or maybe we had caused enough damage for the Empire to be actively hunting us, but when that bounty hunter turned up, I knew there was trouble.|Col Serra to Tionne Solusar years after the battle|<ref name="fug" />}}
A '''skirmish''' took place in [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. Shortly after a [[Battle of the Graveyard|mission to the Graveyard]], the Rebel Alliance's new elite fighting unit, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]], traveled to Ord Mantell to get repairs done on their [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|transport]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], which had sustained heavy damage while fighting at [[the Graveyard]]. Co-led by ex-[[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Col Serra]], Renegade Squadron was relaxing at one of many [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] on Ord Mantell while the repairs were under way when the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]], [[IG-88B|IG-88]], burst into the cantina and attacked the rebels, hoping to collect the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] the Empire had put up on captured Rebels.

Although the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and the local Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]], which IG-88 had notified about the Rebels presence, tried to eliminate Renegade Squadron, the Rebels were able to fight their way back to the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] the ''Falcon'' was stationed at. The Renegades finished the repairs on the ''Falcon'' and eliminated an Imperial [[anti-aircraft gun]] keeping them trapped on Ord Mantell. After holding off the attacking Imperial garrison and defeating IG-88, Renegade Squadron was able to board the ''Falcon'' and leave Ord Mantell.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|My orders were to rendezvous with Han Solo on Ord Mantell for repairs following the damage we sustained in the Alderaan system.|Col Serra on why Renegade Squadron went to Ord Mantell|<ref name="fug" />}}
[[File:OrdMantellCantina.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Renegade Squadron on Ord Mantell.]]
In [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]]—the Alliance's recently established elite fighting force made of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and other [[Fringer/Legends|fringers]]—was sent on a a [[Battle of the Graveyard|mission]] to [[The Graveyard|the remains]] of the destroyed [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to recover a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] that detailed several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] that could host a new Rebel base, following the loss of their [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Co-led by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]-turned Rebel [[Commander/Legends|Commanders]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]], the Renegades were able to secure the holocron, stopping an Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] from capturing its valuable contents.<ref name="fug" />

However, during the fighting, General Solo's personal [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], was badly damaged while searching for the holocron. After Renegade Squadron had retreated with the holocron, [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] ordered Solo and Renegade Squadron to the planet [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] to get repairs for the ''Falcon'' in the meantime. Because the Alliance believed that Ord Mantell was such a backwater planet with a small Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]], Renegade Squadron thought that they would be safe there. However, while the Renegades were relaxing in a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on Ord Mantell, the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[IG-88B|IG-88]], seeking the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] the Empire had posted for captured Rebels, found them. IG-88 burst into the cantina and started firing at the squadron, and soon enough the entire Imperial garrison was made aware of the Renegades' presence on the planet.<ref name="fug" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|Suddenly there were Imperials everywhere. All we had to do was fight our way back to the shipyards, finish the repairs done to our ships and get off Ord Mantell before the nearest Star Destroyer squadron turned up. Simple, right?|Col Serra on the battle|<ref name="fug" />}}
[[File:PIE_4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Renegade Squadron makes it back to the hangar as the Imperials start to attack.]]
As IG-88 started to attack Renegade Squadron, several [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] from the Imperial garrison entered the battle too. However, the Renegades fought back, and most of squadron was able to make their back to the hangar. When they reached the hangar, the squadron discovered that the repairs on the ''Falcon'' had not been completed yet, and that they were temporarily trapped on Ord Mantell. Commander Serra ordered the Renegades to secure the junkyard around the hangar and to find parts there so that Solo could fix the ''Falcon'' himself. After fighting off the assaulting stormtroopers, the Rebels were able to capture the junkyard, using a [[T4-B heavy tank]] they had brought with them as support. [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Solo's [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion and [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], came charging into the battle, ordered by Solo to find parts needed for their ship.<ref name="fug" />

Serra told Renegade Squadron to protect Chewbacca from the assaulting stormtroopers while the Wookiee searched for the parts. The Imperials then launched a larger attack wave, along with [[TX-130T fighter tank]]s and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] from the nearby trading outposts, trying to kill Chewbacca before he could find the parts. The Renegades were still able to hold off the Empire's assault for several minutes, protecting Chewbacca from [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire. Finally, Chewbacca was able to find all the parts he needed among the assorted trash, and ran back to the hangar.<ref name="fug" />

After Chewbacca got to the hangar with the parts, Solo informed his squadron that there was an [[anti-aircraft gun]] on the other side of the junkyard at the trading outpost, which could destroy the ''Falcon'' if they took off. Solo ordered the squadron to go disable the turret at all costs, and Renegade Squadron fought their way to the outpost, pushing past the stormtroopers defending the gun. When Renegade Squadron made it to the outpost though, however, they discovered that the anti-aircraft gun was enveloped by a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]], which was strong enough to repel any attack the Renegades could make against the gun. Commander Serra was quickly able to pinpoint the location of the turret's [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]], at the facilities junk processor, and sent Renegade Squadron to destroy it.<ref name="fug" />

Defeating the stormtroopers guarding the area, the Rebels blew up the generator, disabling the anti-aircraft turret's shield. The Renegades moved back to the trading outpost, and destroyed the now unprotected turret. As soon as the turret was disabled, General Solo informed his [[Squad/Legends|squad]] that the ''Falcon'' needed a new [[Power core/Legends|power core]] to be operational, and that he had located one back over at the processor plant. Serra ordered a member of Renegade Squadron to travel back to the junk processor plant and grab the core, and to bring it back to the hangar. The Rebel ran the power core back to the hangar for Solo to complete his repairs on his ship.<ref name="fug" />

[[File:PIE_2.jpg|thumb|250px|Renegade Squadron fights IG-88 while Solo gets the ''Falcon'' ready for take off.]]
Solo now had all the parts need to repair the ''Falcon'', but he still need time to fix the ships damaged systems. In the meantime, the Imperials had successfully pushed the Renegade Squadron back to the hangar and were now launching one last wave to try to halt the Rebels' retreat. Serra ordered the squadron to hold back the Imperial assault so that Solo could finish his repairs. The Renegades protected the hangar for several minutes, using their T4-B tanks as support again, and the squadron was successfully able to hold back the Imperials as the repairs were completely.<ref name="fug" />

However, as soon as Solo finished his repairs, IG-88 then came into the battle again, leading the remaining Imperial troops, trying to stop Renegade Squadron. Solo ordered the Renegades to destroy the droid and buy enough time for him to get the launch preps for the ''Falcon'' done. Although the bounty hunter [[Droid/Legends|droid]] used a large array of weapons against the Renegades, the rebels were able to badly damage IG-88 and push back the last stormtroopers. Solo then informed his troops that the ''Falcon'' was ready to go, and the rebels boarded the cargo ship and left Ord Mantell for good.<ref name="fug" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|So much for our R&R. We barely made it off Ord Mantell, making the jump to light speed just as those imperial star destroyers turned up. That's when we found out about commander Ackbar. He had been captured while on his recon mission.|Col Serra|<ref name="fug" />}}
The ''Millennium Falcon'' jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] just as a squadron of Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] jumped into the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|system]] to stop them. The remaining members of Renegade Squadron would then rendezvous with Alliance command, were they discovered that [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], the Alliance's [[Mon Calamari (language)/Legends|Mon Calamarian]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], had been captured while on a scouting mission and was now in the Empire's custody. Renegade Squadron was sent to [[Battle of Kessel (0 ABY)|rescue]] Ackbar from the Imperials while the Admiral was being transported to the [[Kessel prison|prison]] colony on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]].<ref name="fug" />

Years later, after the fall of the Empire and the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tionne Solusar]] would track down Col Serra and ask him about Renegade Squadron's history for her [[Jedi Archives/Legends|archives]]. Serra would tell her about many of Renegade Squadron's missions and exploits, including their run in with IG-88 on Ord Mantell.<ref name="fug" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Skirmish on Ord Mantell made its first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'', which recounts the various battles and exploits of Renegade Squadron during the Galactic Civil War. The battle itself makes up the third level in the game's campaign mode.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]''
